fertility_signature_description: Fertile systems satisfy: SR ≥ 0.7 AND NP ≥ 0.7 AND RC ≥ 0.6 AND RD ≥ 0.3 AND OE ≥ 0.6
key_insight: Fertility is partially independent of viability. A system must first be viable, then have specific architectural properties (multiple generative mechanisms + recursive self-modeling) to achieve fertility. The 9-assumption substrate achieves both.
answer_to_director_question: Reality occupies a highly productive region because it has multiple independent generative mechanisms coupled with recursive self-modeling capacity — not because it is merely persistent. Persistence is necessary but insufficient.
